channel.title: DevFocus - 开发者聚焦
channel.description: 每日自动整理的开发者资讯：AI 热榜、GitHub 趋势、技术新闻

item.title: [github_trending] msitarzewski/agency-agents
item.description: Agency-agents is an open-source project that bundles a complete set of AI agents emulating a full-fledged digital agency. It includes roles like frontend wizards, Reddit community managers, and whimsy injectors, allowing developers to assemble a tailored AI team with minimal configuration. The beauty lies in its plug-and-play nature — you don't need to train each agent from scratch. For indie hackers, startup founders, and content creators, this means rapidly prototyping products or automating o

item.title: [github_trending] iptv-org/iptv
item.description: The iptv-org/iptv project is an open-source collection of publicly available IPTV channels from around the world. It allows users to access thousands of live TV streams directly through IP addresses, bypassing traditional cable or satellite subscriptions. The repository is community-driven and regularly updated, making it a valuable resource for cord-cutters and international viewers. However, users should be aware of potential copyright issues and ensure they comply with local laws. This projec

item.title: [github_trending] OpenCut-app/OpenCut
item.description: OpenCut is an open-source video editor designed as a free alternative to CapCut. It offers powerful editing features without any subscription or watermark, making it ideal for content creators who want full control over their tools. The project is cross-platform and community-driven, ensuring transparency and continuous improvement. For anyone tired of paid video editors or restrictive platforms, OpenCut provides a flexible and privacy-respecting solution.

item.title: [github_trending] ocornut/imgui
item.description: Dear ImGui is a bloat-free graphical user interface library for C++ with minimal dependencies, offering a seamless immediate mode GUI experience. It is especially popular among game developers and tool builders who need quick, interactive panels for debugging or real-time data visualization without the overhead of traditional UI frameworks. Its focus on simplicity and performance makes it a go-to choice for indie games and embedded tools.

item.title: [github_trending] asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks
item.description: This GitHub repository compiles leaked system prompts from popular AI models like Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, as well as OpenAI's ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking and GPT 5.5 Instant. By exposing the hidden instructions that shape how these models behave, it offers rare insight into prompt engineering, safety guardrails, and role-setting techniques. AI researchers and developers can learn how top models are orchestrated, but the leak also raises important questions about access control and inte

item.title: [github_trending] Panniantong/Agent-Reach
item.description: Agent-Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents the ability to 'see' the entire internet. It enables reading and searching across major platforms like Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu — all without any API fees. The biggest pain point for AI agent developers is getting real-time, cross-platform data; Agent-Reach solves this with a single command. Whether you're building a social media analyzer, a tech trend tracker, or a content aggregator, you can now equip your AI wit

item.title: [36kr] 惊天巨骗，一夜刷爆全球榜单的「神秘实验室」，竟然是假的
item.description: A mysterious 'lab' project recently topped global tech charts overnight, only to be exposed as an elaborate scam. The project claimed groundbreaking technological achievements, attracting massive attention and investment, but had no real product. This incident has sparked widespread discussion about tech investment bubbles and ranking manipulation, serving as a cautionary tale for the industry to strengthen due diligence. It highlights the dangers of hype and the need for critical evaluation in

item.title: [github_trending] jamiepine/voicebox
item.description: Voicebox is an open-source AI voice studio that lets you clone voices, dictate text, and create speech content effortlessly. It runs entirely offline, ensuring privacy, and can generate realistic voice output from just a few minutes of audio samples, even making the AI say things it never originally said. Podcasters, voice actors, content creators, and visually impaired users will find it invaluable for production and accessibility, significantly lowering the barrier to professional-grade voice

item.title: [github_trending] usestrix/strix
item.description: Strix is an open-source AI-powered penetration testing tool designed to automatically identify and fix vulnerabilities in applications. By simulating cyber attacks with artificial intelligence, it helps developers catch security flaws early in the development cycle, significantly reducing manual effort and cost. This tool is particularly valuable for startups and individual developers who lack dedicated security teams, offering a practical way to strengthen app security without complex infrastru

item.title: [github_trending] rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
item.description: This GitHub repository offers a hands-on curriculum for aspiring AI engineers, covering everything from foundations to deployment. The motto 'Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others' reflects its practical approach: you don't just study theory, you create real products. Perfect for beginners or career switchers who want a structured path into AI engineering.

item.title: [github_trending] calesthio/OpenMontage
item.description: OpenMontage is the world's first open-source, agentic video production system, featuring 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and over 500 agent skills. It transforms your AI coding assistant into a full-fledged video production studio, automating tasks like scriptwriting, editing, voiceover, and effects. This solves the complexity and high cost of traditional video creation, enabling anyone to produce professional videos using natural language commands. Content creators, marketers, and developers benefit fr

item.title: [36kr] DeepSeek V4「满血版」曝光了，最快明天发布
item.description: The 'full-blooded' version of DeepSeek V4 has been leaked, potentially launching as soon as tomorrow. This update is rumored to bring significant performance improvements, including enhanced reasoning capabilities and faster response times. As a key representative of Chinese AI large language models, each DeepSeek release attracts intense interest. The V4 full version is expected to surpass existing models on multiple benchmarks, propelling domestic AI technology forward. This update matters bec

item.title: [36kr] K3发布48小时，服务器满载、英伟达暴跌、Anthropic连夜改订阅
item.description: Just 48 hours after the launch of K3, the new product caused industry turmoil: servers were overwhelmed by demand, Nvidia's stock plummeted, and Anthropic hastily revised its subscription model. Likely an AI chip or computing platform built on a novel architecture, K3's powerful performance has disrupted the existing market landscape. This event demonstrates how hardware innovation can rapidly shift competitive dynamics, forcing investors and competitors to pay close attention. It underscores th

item.title: [36kr] 8点1氪丨美国将对加拿大部分产品加征50%关税；任泽平回应会员炒股亏损千万；韩国今年人均GDP有望达3.9万美元
item.description: This morning's news digest: The US announced a 50% tariff on certain Canadian products, escalating trade tensions; renowned economist Ren Zeping responded to allegations that his members lost millions in stock trading, sparking public debate; South Korea's per capita GDP is forecast to reach $39,000 this year, signaling a strong economic recovery. These stories cover international trade, investment, and macroeconomics, each carrying significant implications. The tariff move could impact supply c

item.title: [36kr] 掌管Codex额度的「赛博义父」，到底什么来头
item.description: A mysterious figure known as the "Cyber Godfather" has emerged in developer circles, controlling the allocation of OpenAI Codex API quotas. This gatekeeper holds immense influence over access to AI-powered code generation, effectively deciding who gets to leverage this transformative tool. The article delves into his background, the source of his power, and his real impact on the developer ecosystem, revealing the hidden human network behind AI resource distribution.

item.title: [36kr] 今年最火爆的三部短剧，没有一个是“正经团队”
item.description: This year's three biggest short drama hits—"Peerless", "Escape from the British Museum", and "29"—were all produced by non-traditional teams. Emerging from short-video creators, self-media influencers, and amateur collectives, they defeated professional studios through precise emotional marketing, unconventional scripts, and a low-cost, high-turnaround model. This signals the arrival of an era where anyone can create viral content, forcing traditional TV and film companies to rethink their compe

item.title: [36kr] 2026 IMO中国满分屠榜，上海中学横扫金牌，GPT-5.6再现AlphaGO时刻
item.description: At the 2026 International Mathematical Olympiad, China's team achieved a perfect score, sweeping the gold medals, with students from Shanghai High School dominating the podium. Meanwhile, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 achieved a perfect score on the same exam for the first time, a milestone comparable to AlphaGo defeating Lee Sedol. This is not only a breakthrough in AI mathematical reasoning but also sparks profound discussions about human-machine collaboration and the future of education.

item.title: [36kr] WAIC 2026终极盘点：15大核心趋势，看透AI下半场
item.description: As the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) concluded, a comprehensive review identified 15 core trends, including the commercial deployment of multimodal models, autonomous decision-making of AI agents, the explosion of on-device intelligence, embodied robotics, and AI safety governance. The report declares that AI has moved from exploration into the second half of full-scale application, where competition will focus not on model parameters but on scenario penetration and ecosys

item.title: [36kr] 冲击30亿票房的《功夫女足》，谁才是最大的赢家？
item.description: "Kung Fu Women's Football" is on track to smash the 3 billion yuan box office barrier, becoming a phenomenon of the year. Who benefits most from this feast? Beyond the director and lead actors, the venture capitalists who backed it, the short-video platform co-producer, and even the sports IP itself stand to gain long-term returns far exceeding ticket sales. The article dissects the profit distribution across content creation, marketing, and distribution, revealing the business logic behind a bl

item.title: [36kr] 特斯拉给小孩做了辆没AI的脚蹬车：200刀，卖爆了
item.description: Tesla released a pedal-powered kids' vehicle priced at just $200, strikingly without any AI features. It focuses on pure cycling fun, no charging or smart interactions needed, yet it has become a hot seller. This success shows that sometimes simplicity and practicality resonate more with consumers, especially parents seeking an outdoor activity for their children.

item.title: [infoq] 边端AI不只缺算力：安谋科技重做CPU、NPU、VPU与AI操作系统
item.description: Arm China realized that the bottleneck of edge AI goes beyond computing power, so they redesigned CPU, NPU, VPU, and the AI operating system from scratch. The new architecture emphasizes hardware-software co-design to improve local intelligent processing efficiency and energy efficiency, especially for IoT and smart devices. This allows developers to deploy AI inference with lower power and latency, unlocking the true potential of edge computing.

item.title: [infoq] WAIC 2026现场，阡视科技发布超节点系统，专为Token工厂而生
item.description: At WAIC 2026, Qianshi Technology unveiled a supernode system designed specifically for Token factories—large-scale text generation scenarios. The system leverages dedicated hardware and software co-optimization to reduce inference latency and boost throughput. For companies relying on AI content generation and cloud services, this translates to lower computing costs and faster response times, providing critical support for high-concurrency text generation.

item.title: [infoq] 寻找 AI 原生时代创业者：极客部落 OPC 入驻招募启动！
item.description: Geeks Tribe OPC has officially launched its recruitment for entrepreneurs in the AI-native era, a community and incubation platform tailored for tech founders. It offers resource matching, technical exchange, and coworking spaces to help AI geeks turn ideas into products. For AI talents seeking partners, funding, or mentorship, this is a rare opportunity to quickly plug into the ecosystem and accelerate project delivery.

item.title: [infoq] 企业 AI：方法论易得，交付力难求
item.description: A frustrating gap has emerged in enterprise AI: methodologies, frameworks, and best practices are everywhere, but high-quality, deliverable AI projects remain rare. The article points out that the real bottleneck lies in delivery execution rather than algorithms. This serves as a wake-up call for business leaders and tech teams, urging them to prioritize engineering capabilities and project management—otherwise, even the best methodologies remain just talk.

item.title: [infoq] Agent 会执行，但谁来帮它记住过去？
item.description: This article tackles a critical challenge in AI agent development: memory retention. While agents can execute tasks effectively, they often struggle to recall past interactions or long-term context. The piece explores solutions like persistent state management, vector databases, and memory modules that enable agents to remember and adapt over time. This capability is essential for building reliable autonomous systems that handle multi-step workflows coherently, making it a must-read for develope

item.title: [infoq] 为敏感的云系统设计连续授权
item.description: This article introduces continuous authorization for sensitive cloud systems, moving beyond static one-time permissions. It dynamically adjusts access rights based on real-time user behavior, context, and risk signals, effectively mitigating insider threats and privilege abuse. This approach is especially valuable for industries like finance, healthcare, and government that require stringent security and compliance, offering a practical implementation path under a zero-trust architecture.

item.title: [infoq] Grab构建安全的智能体AI工作负载平台
item.description: Grab, a Southeast Asian tech giant, details its journey to build a secure platform for AI agent workloads. The platform employs sandboxing, fine-grained access control, and comprehensive audit logging to ensure autonomous agents operate safely in production. It also supports dynamic scaling and lifecycle management, providing a security infrastructure blueprint for enterprises deploying AI assistants at scale. This is especially relevant for applications with strict trust and compliance requirem

item.title: [infoq] 一句话上线 AI Agent 应用：火山 Supabase + IGA Pages 全栈部署实践
item.description: This article demonstrates a full-stack deployment practice using Volcengine's Supabase database and IGA Pages static hosting to launch an AI agent application with just one command or configuration file. It dramatically reduces the deployment barrier, enabling developers to quickly move from prototype to production with interactive AI assistants. This approach is ideal for teams seeking rapid experimentation and cost-effective rollout of agent-based applications.

item.title: [infoq] 谷歌发布A2UI v0.9：可移植、框架无关的生成式UI
item.description: Google released A2UI v0.9, a portable and framework-agnostic generative UI toolkit. It lets developers build dynamic user interfaces declaratively without being locked into a specific frontend framework, significantly boosting cross-platform reusability and development speed. This is particularly useful for creating AI-driven interfaces that need to adapt quickly across different environments, offering a new paradigm for frontend development in the age of generative AI.

item.title: [infoq] 专访孙元浩：乘坐星环号，把数据库“移民火星”的人
item.description: In this interview, Sun Yuanhao, founder of Transwarp, discusses his vision of 'migrating databases to Mars' — a metaphor for moving legacy database systems to modern, cloud-native architectures. He explains Transwarp's innovations in distributed databases and data cloud platforms, and how they help enterprises smoothly transition their data infrastructure. The piece highlights China's growing ambition in database technology and offers insights into how domestic companies can leapfrog traditional

item.title: [hackernews] Show HN: I replaced a $120k bowling center system with $1,600 in ESP32s
item.description: A developer replaced a $120,000 bowling center management system with just $1,600 worth of ESP32 microcontrollers. The project demonstrates how low-cost hardware and clever software design can handle core functions like scoring, sensor integration, and user management. For small bowling alley operators or embedded systems enthusiasts, this is an inspiring example of how to achieve professional-grade automation on a shoestring budget.

item.title: [hackernews] Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
item.description: Kimi K3 is an open-source frontier intelligence model designed to democratize state-of-the-art AI capabilities. It tackles the problem of closed-source AI monopolies by allowing developers and researchers to freely experiment, customize, and improve the model. Startups and academic institutions, in particular, can build their own intelligent applications without expensive licensing fees. This open approach is expected to accelerate AI innovation and democratization, making advanced artificial in

item.title: [hackernews] AWS: Inaccurate Estimated Billing Data – $1.7 billion
item.description: AWS has admitted to a massive inaccuracy in its estimated billing data, with errors reaching a staggering $1.7 billion. This means many customers may have received wildly incorrect billing estimates, affecting their budget planning and resource allocation decisions. While AWS assures that final charges will be based on precise data, such a large discrepancy undermines trust in cloud cost management tools. For enterprises heavily reliant on AWS, this event highlights the risks of depending solely

item.title: [hackernews] China’s open-weights AI strategy is winning
item.description: This analysis argues that China's strategy of releasing open-weight AI models is proving to be a winning approach against the more closed models preferred by many US companies. By openly sharing weights of powerful large language models, Chinese firms like Alibaba and Baidu attract global developers who can fine-tune and deploy these models freely. This accelerates innovation and adoption, lowers barriers for small businesses and researchers, and could shift the balance of AI power. The piece of

item.title: [hackernews] Qwen 3.8
item.description: Qwen 3.8 is the latest open-source large language model from Alibaba's Qwen family, boasting over 38 billion parameters. It delivers significant improvements in mathematical reasoning, code generation, and multilingual comprehension, with faster inference speed and stronger support for both Chinese and English. Because it remains fully open-source, developers, researchers, and enterprises can deploy it locally or fine-tune it for customized tasks. This makes Qwen 3.8 a compelling choice for buil

item.title: [producthunt] OpenSEO
item.description: OpenSEO is an open-source SEO toolkit marketed as a free alternative to Ahrefs. It includes website crawling, keyword research, backlink analysis, and rank tracking, all running locally so no subscription or third-party service is needed. This empowers small business owners, independent bloggers, and budget-conscious marketers to access professional-level SEO insights at zero cost, helping them discover content opportunities, monitor competitors, and boost organic traffic. Because the community

item.title: [hackernews] Transcribe.cpp
item.description: Transcribe.cpp is a lightweight speech-to-text tool written in C++ that leverages OpenAI's Whisper model with extensive optimizations for CPU-only devices, including Raspberry Pis. It runs entirely offline, starts up quickly, and consumes minimal memory, making it ideal for privacy-conscious users, journalists in remote areas, or developers needing on-device transcription without cloud dependencies. By eliminating the need for a GPU or internet connection, Transcribe.cpp solves the latency and c

item.title: [hackernews] Regressive JPEGs
item.description: Regressive JPEG introduces a novel image encoding technique that works in reverse: it starts with a low-quality version and progressively discards more data to shrink file sizes, opposite to traditional progressive JPEG. This is particularly useful for fast previews on limited bandwidth, as it allows decoders to quickly show a rough image and refine later. Best of all, it remains fully compatible with existing JPEG decoders, requiring no new software. It's a clever optimization for web performan

item.title: [hackernews] Airport Simulator
item.description: Airport Simulator is a detailed simulation tool that puts you in charge of a busy airport, managing everything from flight scheduling and runway allocation to gate assignments and baggage handling. More than just a game, it serves as an educational platform for aviation management, offering realistic physics and real-time decision-making challenges. Users get an immersive look into the coordination and pressure behind airport operations. It's a valuable resource for aviation enthusiasts, student

item.title: [hackernews] Claude Fable produced a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture
item.description: Claude, under a framework called Fable, has reportedly produced a counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture, a long-standing open problem in algebraic geometry. The conjecture asserts that a polynomial map with non-zero constant Jacobian is invertible, and despite many partial proofs, no general counterexample had been found. Claude's construction, if validated, would be a significant mathematical breakthrough. This news also highlights how advanced AI models can contribute to frontier research

item.title: [producthunt] ZooData
item.description: ZooData is a dedicated data layer built specifically for AI agents, addressing the fragmentation and permission headaches that agents face when accessing data from multiple sources. It offers a unified API with real-time data streaming, enabling AI agents to read, write, and sync structured data seamlessly across different systems. Think of it as giving your agent a nervous system for data. For developers and enterprises building complex AI workflows, ZooData cuts down the heavy lifting of data

item.title: [hackernews] The Kimi K3 Moment
item.description: The Kimi K3 Moment marks a significant upgrade for the Kimi AI assistant, introducing enhanced reasoning and multimodal understanding. This update transforms how users interact with long documents, code, and images, making conversations feel more natural and productive. It shifts large language models from casual chat toys into serious work companions, particularly benefiting researchers, legal professionals, and investors who need deep information processing.

item.title: [producthunt] Fuzzy AI
item.description: Fuzzy AI is a prospect warming tool that uses artificial intelligence to engage potential customers in natural conversations before you reach out. It solves the problem of cold outreach by building interest and trust gradually, making the first touchpoint more effective. B2B sales teams benefit most, as Fuzzy AI transforms awkward cold emails into warm, welcomed interactions, significantly boosting reply rates and conversions.

item.title: [producthunt] Clark
item.description: Clark is an AI coworker equipped with its own cloud computer, moving beyond simple chatbots to become an agent that can independently operate desktop software, browse the web, and manage files. Running entirely in the cloud, Clark handles complex tasks like compiling reports, sorting emails, or using SaaS tools, then returns the finished work to you. For teams drowning in repetitive office chores, Clark acts as a tireless digital employee that can control existing software without needing API in

item.title: [producthunt] Nautis
item.description: Nautis is an AI-native operating system designed specifically for founders, integrating project management, CRM, financial tracking, and more. Its AI generates insights and suggests next steps automatically, freeing founders from administrative overhead so they can focus on strategy. Early-stage startup teams benefit most, as Nautis becomes the single brain that keeps everything aligned and actionable.

item.title: [producthunt] Loova Ads Studio
item.description: Loova Ads Studio is an AI-powered ad creation tool that goes beyond aesthetics to optimize for conversion. It automatically tests different copy, images, and audience segments, then recommends the highest-performing combination. Small businesses and marketers benefit most, as Loova acts like a dedicated ad optimizer without requiring design expertise, helping them achieve better ROI with less effort.

item.title: [producthunt] Replay QA
item.description: Replay QA is an intelligent quality monitoring tool that records user session replays and automatically analyzes them for anomalies, catching bugs before users report them. It shifts the approach from reactive firefighting to proactive prevention, reducing the impact of issues on customers. Development and QA teams benefit most, as Replay QA acts like a round-the-clock sentinel, ensuring problems are spotted and fixed early.

item.title: [producthunt] LiveDemo
item.description: LiveDemo is an open-source interactive demo platform designed as a free alternative to paid tools like Storylane, Navattic, and Arcade. It lets you create product tours, simulated walkthroughs, and interactive demos with full code transparency and control. For SaaS companies and product teams, LiveDemo means no vendor lock-in, the ability to tailor demo experiences exactly to their needs, and significant cost savings on licensing. The open-source community can also contribute improvements, makin

item.title: [producthunt] Spycost
item.description: Spycost is a price-tracking and discount verification tool that helps shoppers determine whether a sale is genuine. By simply pasting a product URL, it displays the item's historical price chart, showing the lowest, average, and current prices, and flags inflated original prices and fake discount percentages. For frequent online shoppers who often fall for limited-time deals, Spycost provides transparency before checkout, revealing the true value of a product and preventing impulse buys driven b

item.title: [juejin] Codex 穷鬼大救星
item.description: This article introduces a budget-friendly AI coding workflow called "Poor Guy Workflow," aimed at helping developers with limited funds leverage tools like Codex to complete projects at low cost. The author humorously dubs it an "AI shady contractor team," emphasizing that it allows coders on a tight budget to enjoy AI efficiency boosts without expensive subscriptions. It's especially practical for indie developers and small teams.

item.title: [producthunt] Skippr AI
item.description: Skippr AI is a live AI employee that lives inside your product, serving every user in real time. It solves the problem of slow customer support and high labor costs by turning your product itself into a conversational assistant. Whether answering questions, guiding users, or collecting feedback, Skippr interacts naturally like a real team member. For SaaS products, e-commerce platforms, or any app requiring user guidance and support, it dramatically improves user experience and operational effic

item.title: [juejin] 小程序上线半年我赚了多少钱？
item.description: An indie developer shared the real revenue and operational data of his expense tracking mini-program called 'Fuzhu' over six months since launch. He iterated from v1.0.0 to v1.6.1, documenting development logs, user growth, and profitability. This article demonstrates the possibility of building a product in spare time, offering a complete postmortem from coding to business. It’s especially useful for programmers exploring side projects.

item.title: [juejin] Cursor 转 Codex 大半个月，聊聊我的真实感受
item.description: After switching from Cursor to Amazon Codex for over half a month, a developer shared their real experience of the fundamental differences. While Cursor first impressed them with AI-assisted coding, Codex felt like a true super agent that can independently execute tasks, not just an assistant. The author believes Codex represents the next phase of AI programming—evolving from conversational completion to proactive agency. This comparison is invaluable for developers seeking the cutting edge in A

item.title: [juejin] 写给年轻程序员的几点建议-2
item.description: The author offers advice for young programmers in the post-internet-bonus era, emphasizing the need to develop business acumen and writing skills. Given China's huge population, successfully selling a product or writing a viral WeChat article can bring significant wealth. The article argues that market insight and content communication are as crucial as technical skills, making it relevant for early-career programmers looking to diversify their income streams.

item.title: [juejin] 程序员副业 | 2026年4月复盘
item.description: A programmer shares their side project recap for April 2026, responding to readers who urged an update. The author describes an intense period of coding overnight after the new year, finally releasing a new version of their software. This post highlights the significant time commitment and self-discipline required for side projects, along with the fleeting relief of shipping a release. For developers juggling side hustles, this recap resonates deeply—it shows that sustained effort eventually pay

item.title: [juejin] 面试官问你：“AI 能写 80% 的代码了，公司为什么还需要你？”
item.description: The article explores how technical interviews have transformed as AI now writes 80% of the code. Interviewers assume proficiency with AI tools and instead evaluate skills that make developers irreplaceable: problem decomposition, business understanding, architectural thinking, and effective human-AI collaboration. It cautions against over-reliance on AI-generated code and urges developers to cultivate uniquely human competencies for long-term career relevance.

item.title: [juejin] 停更的这一年，All In AI、陪媳妇生娃、还考了个研
item.description: A Juejin author returns after a year-long hiatus, recounting a whirlwind period of diving headfirst into AI, supporting his wife through childbirth, and even squeezing in graduate school exams. Through first-person narration, he shares how he balanced career, family, and personal growth, offering a relatable and inspiring tale for developers navigating life’s major milestones while striving to keep improving.

item.title: [juejin] 🚀 2026 年 6 月 GitHub 十大热门项目排行榜 🔥
item.description: This article highlights the top ten trending open source projects on GitHub in June 2026, organized around five key themes: agent perception, skill marketplaces, video production, code intelligence, and parallel orchestration. The projects showcase cutting-edge developments in AI agents, decentralized skill trading platforms, AI-powered video generation, smart code completion and review tools, and high-performance parallel scheduling frameworks. For developers who want to stay ahead of the open

item.title: [juejin] 前后端一起消失：2026 年 7 月，前端正在被彻底“重新定义”
item.description: This provocative article argues that by July 2026, breakthroughs like Google's leaked Gemini 3.5 Pro will erase the traditional boundary between frontend and backend development. AI capable of generating both UI and business logic could make today's specialized roles obsolete, forcing developers to rethink their skill sets. It asks what 'full-stack' means when machines handle the stack, and explores how coders can adapt to a world where the very concept of frontend is being redefined.

item.title: [juejin] 2026编程圈很火的10个Skills
item.description: The 10 hottest Skills in programming circles for 2026 refer not to developers' own abilities but to standardized workflow modules for AI coding assistants. These skill packages transform AI from random code generation to structured delivery, covering code review, test generation, documentation, and more. For teams and individual developers exploring AI-assisted programming, mastering these skills is becoming essential for boosting productivity and code quality.

item.title: [zhihu] 为什么豆包没有被 DeepSeek「斩杀」？
item.description: A popular Zhihu discussion explores why ByteDance's Doubao AI hasn't been 'killed' by DeepSeek. The key insight is that the two products have different positioning: Doubao focuses on multimodal, everyday scenarios while DeepSeek excels in deep reasoning. They aren't direct competitors. Doubao leverages ByteDance's massive user base and scene integration to retain users. Its continuous iteration and ecosystem lock-in make it hard to replace. This shows that in the AI landscape, differentiation of

item.title: [zhihu] OpenAI 负责人称 Kimi 开源是减速主义，他的说法有道理吗？开源对 AI 的发展能带来什么？
item.description: An OpenAI executive called Kimi open-sourcing 'decelerationism,' sparking debate on Zhihu. Supporters argue that open-source accelerates technology democratization, fosters innovation, and enables security audits, while critics warn of misuse and fragmentation. Kimi's open-source release lowers barriers for SMEs and researchers. For AI, open-source acts like Linux did for operating systems—it drives community collaboration but may slow down proprietary giants. Overall, the benefits of openness s

item.title: [zhihu] 如何看待追光总裁称《三国第一部：争洛阳》以曹操为主角，是参考了 DeepSeek 的建议?
item.description: The president of Light Chaser Animation revealed that the decision to make Cao Cao the protagonist in their new film 'Three Kingdoms: Battle for Luoyang' was influenced by DeepSeek AI. This sparked discussions on Zhihu. The AI analyzed historical texts, market data, and audience preferences, suggesting that Cao Cao offers stronger dramatic potential. The producer sees it as a creative aid, not a replacement. It showcases how AI can assist cultural industries—from market analysis to character sel

item.title: [zhihu] 如何评价代数几何著名猜想「雅可比猜想」被 AI 证伪？如此简洁、可独立验证的数学发现意味着什么？
item.description: The famous Jacobian conjecture in algebraic geometry has been falsified by an AI, sending shockwaves through the mathematical community. Using symbolic reasoning and exhaustive search, the AI found a counterexample that is simple and independently verifiable. This is not just about one conjecture—it signals that AI can act as a 'discoverer' in mathematical research, rapidly screening structures humans might miss. For mathematicians and AI researchers, this foreshadows a new paradigm where AI bec

item.title: [zhihu] 为什么「强化学习之父」理查德·萨顿认为，如今的大模型仍然不是真正的智能？
item.description: Richard Sutton, widely regarded as the 'father of reinforcement learning,' recently argued that today's large language models still fall short of true intelligence. He believes these models merely excel at pattern matching on vast datasets, lacking genuine understanding of causal structures, self-awareness, and the ability to interact actively with the environment. Sutton emphasizes that real intelligence requires goal-directed learning and exploration—core principles of reinforcement learning.

item.title: [zhihu] 如何看待最近网传的 DeepSeek v4 正式版灰测？
item.description: Rumors have been circulating online that DeepSeek is conducting grayscale testing of its official fourth-generation large model, called v4. Though the company has not officially confirmed it, some users report experiencing improved reasoning capabilities and lower latency. The buzz quickly spread across the AI community, as DeepSeek is known for its cost-effective and open-source models. If the v4 upgrade is real, it could lower the barrier to entry for powerful AI, benefiting developers, SMEs,

item.title: 📅 历史归档
item.description: 查看往日精选与趋势变化
